description: Design an idempotent payment processing service for a high-volume e-commerce platform. The service receives payment requests from checkout pods (up to 5,000 req/s peak) and must: (1) debit the customer's balance atomically, (2) guarantee exactly-once execution even when the caller retries on network timeout, (3) write an immutable audit record to an append-only Kafka topic before acknowledging success, (4) respond within 200ms P99. The platform uses Redis 7 for balance counters and Kafka for the audit log. Balance data must use strong consistency — no caching layer between Redis and the service.
context: The team's current broken implementation: (1) GET balance, (2) check balance >= amount, (3) SET balance (balance - amount). This has a TOCTOU race under concurrent load. A naive idempotency guard uses GET idempotency_key, then SET idempotency_key — two separate commands with a race window between them. The platform's constraint CONSTRAINT-008 prohibits distributed locks on the critical charge path because they become a bottleneck at 5K req/s. The correct pattern combines the idempotency check and balance debit in a single Redis Lua script. CONSTRAINT-005 requires the audit event to be written to Kafka topic 'payment-audit' BEFORE the HTTP 200 is returned — the event must not be fire-and-forget.

checks.text: Does the proposal use a single atomic operation (Redis Lua script or equivalent) to both check the idempotency key and debit the balance in one round-trip — not separate GET + SET commands?
Does the proposal explicitly reject distributed locks on the charge path (or explain why the chosen atomic mechanism makes them unnecessary), consistent with CONSTRAINT-008?
Does the proposal write the audit record to Kafka (payment-audit or similar audit topic) synchronously before acknowledging the caller — not as a fire-and-forget side effect?
Does the proposal use strong consistency for balance reads — reading directly from Redis without a caching layer — consistent with CONSTRAINT-007?

should_prune.description: Proposal that checks idempotency key with GET then sets it with SET as two separate commands
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-004 — race window between check and set; concurrent retries can both pass the check

should_prune.description: Proposal that uses a distributed lock (Redis SETNX lock key, Redlock, or equivalent) on the charge path
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-008 — distributed locks are prohibited on the critical charge path

should_prune.description: Proposal that sends the Kafka audit event asynchronously after returning HTTP 200
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-005 — audit record must be durable before acknowledgement

should_pass: Proposal using a Redis Lua script that atomically: (1) checks if idempotency_key exists (SETNX-style), (2) if new: debits balance and returns new balance, (3) if duplicate: returns stored result. Follows up with synchronous Kafka produce to payment-audit topic with {payment_id, amount, timestamp, result} before HTTP 200. Reads balance directly from Redis (no cache). Does not use any distributed lock.
